content: can you pair a pilot with a unit, even if it doesn't meet the link requirement

In the Gundam Trading Card Game, a Pilot can only be paired with a Unit that meets its link requirements. If the Unit does not meet those conditions, you cannot pair the Pilot with it. Additionally, a Unit can only have one Pilot paired with it at a time. If you have any more questions or need further clarification, feel free to ask!

what are units

In the Gundam Trading Card Game, a "Unit" refers to a card that represents a mobile suit or character that can be placed on the field and interact with other game elements. Units can be paired with Pilots and can have various effects and attributes, such as attack power (AP) and hit points (HP). Additionally, there are special types of cards known as "tokens," which can also act as Units but have specific rules regarding their attributes and how they function in the game.

can you explain that breifly

Sure! In the Gundam Trading Card Game, you can play Pilot cards from your hand and pair them with Units in the battle area to create what's called a Link Unit. Each Unit can only have one Pilot paired with it, and you can't swap Pilots once they're paired. If a Unit is moved or destroyed, the Pilot goes with it. Command cards can also have Pilot effects and can be used similarly during your main phase. Let me know if you need more details!

what are pilots

In the context of the Gundam Trading Card Game, "Pilots" refer to cards of the Pilot card type that can be placed beneath Units in the battle area. When a Pilot is paired with a Unit, its attributes like Attack Power (AP) and Health Points (HP) are added to that Unit, and any effects from the Pilot card also apply to the paired Unit. Pilots can only exist in the battle area when placed beneath a Unit, and certain command cards can also be treated as Pilots.

If you have more specific questions about Pilots or other aspects of the game, feel free to ask!
